# GitHub Discussions Categories Guide

This document outlines the categories for GitHub Discussions in the Nexus IDE repository. Use these categories to organize conversations and help the community find relevant discussions.

---

## Category Overview

### 1. 📢 General
**For:** General announcements, news, and community updates

**Use this category for:**
- Release announcements
- Feature highlights
- Blog posts and articles about Nexus IDE
- Upcoming events or announcements
- General project news

**Example topics:**
- "Nexus IDE v1.0.0 is officially released!"
- "New AI provider support coming soon"
- "Roadmap update for Q3 2026"

---

### 2. ❓ Help
**For:** Questions, troubleshooting, and general support

**Use this category for:**
- Setup and installation questions
- How-to guides and tutorials
- Troubleshooting advice
- Configuration help
- General "how do I..." questions
- Performance optimization tips

**Example topics:**
- "How do I set up SSH for Git in Nexus IDE?"
- "AI completions not working - need help"
- "How to configure multiple AI providers?"
- "Portable vs installer - which should I use?"

**Note:** This is NOT for bug reports (use Issues tab instead)

---

### 3. 🐛 Bug Reports
**For:** Discussing known bugs and workarounds

**Use this category for:**
- Confirmed bugs and their workarounds
- Discussion of bug fixes
- Temporary solutions while awaiting patches
- Regression discussions
- Testing reports for bug fixes

**Example topics:**
- "Workaround for Git authentication on Windows"
- "Discussion: Performance issues on older machines"
- "Testing the v1.0.1 beta patch for crash recovery"

**Note:** To report a new bug, use the [Issues tab](https://github.com/Joelito080/nexus-ide/issues) with the bug report template

---

### 4. 💡 Feature Requests
**For:** Discussing feature ideas and improvements

**Use this category for:**
- Ideas for new features
- Discussions about feature implementations
- Use case sharing
- Feature roadmap discussion
- Integration suggestions
- Plugin ideas

**Example topics:**
- "Thoughts on collaborative editing features?"
- "Would you like database integration?"
- "Discussion: REST client integration"
- "Community input on v1.1 roadmap"

**Note:** To formally request a feature, use the [Issues tab](https://github.com/Joelito080/nexus-ide/issues) with the feature request template

---

### 5. 🎨 Showcase
**For:** Sharing projects, workflows, and creative uses

**Use this category for:**
- Show and tell of projects built with Nexus IDE
- Workflows and productivity tips
- Extensions and plugins you've created
- Community contributions
- Before/after productivity improvements
- "Look what I built!" posts

**Example topics:**
- "Built an AI-powered code generator with Nexus IDE"
- "My developer workflow using Nexus IDE"
- "Custom theme I created for the community"
- "How Nexus IDE improved my productivity"

---

### 6. 🧩 Plugins & Extensions
**For:** Discussing plugins, extensions, and integrations

**Use this category for:**
- Plugin recommendations and reviews
- Extension development discussions
- Plugin compatibility issues
- Marketplace feedback
- Custom integration ideas
- Plugin development help

**Example topics:**
- "Is there a plugin for X functionality?"
- "I created a plugin for Docker support"
- "Plugin recommendations for web development"
- "How to develop your own Nexus IDE plugin"

**Note:** For plugin bugs, use the Issues tab

---

## Discussion Guidelines

### Do ✅

- **Be respectful**: Treat other community members with courtesy
- **Be clear**: Provide context and details for your discussion
- **Search first**: Check existing discussions before creating duplicates
- **Stay on topic**: Keep discussions focused on the category
- **Provide examples**: Share code, screenshots, or use cases when relevant
- **Follow up**: Provide feedback when others help you
- **Help others**: Share your knowledge with the community

### Don't ❌

- **Report bugs here**: Use [Issues](https://github.com/Joelito080/nexus-ide/issues) for bug reports
- **Spam**: Don't promote unrelated products or services
- **Off-topic**: Keep discussions related to Nexus IDE
- **Harassment**: No harassment, hate speech, or discrimination
- **Commercial spam**: Don't use discussions for commercial advertising
- **Support for other products**: This is for Nexus IDE support only

---

## Category Selection Guide

**Not sure which category to use? Try this:**

```
Do you have a question or need help?
├─ YES → Help ❓
└─ NO → Continue

Are you reporting a bug?
├─ YES → Use Issues tab (not Discussions)
└─ NO → Continue

Are you suggesting a new feature?
├─ YES → Feature Requests 💡 (or use Issues for formal request)
└─ NO → Continue

Are you sharing a project or workflow?
├─ YES → Showcase 🎨
└─ NO → Continue

Are you discussing plugins?
├─ YES → Plugins & Extensions 🧩
└─ NO → Continue

Is it general news or announcements?
├─ YES → General 📢
└─ NO → Choose closest match or ask in Help ❓
```

---

## Best Practices

### Title Guidelines

Good titles are:
- ✅ Specific: "How to configure Ollama with Nexus IDE"
- ✅ Clear: "Using Git SSH keys for GitHub authentication"
- ✅ Concise: "Dark theme recommendations?" (vs. "theme")

Avoid:
- ❌ Generic: "Help me", "Question", "Does anyone know?"
- ❌ Unclear: "Not working", "Problems", "Support needed"
- ❌ All caps: "HOW DO I...", "URGENT HELP NEEDED"

### Content Guidelines

Include:
- Context about what you're trying to accomplish
- Steps you've already tried
- Version information (Nexus IDE version, OS, etc.)
- Screenshots or code examples (when relevant)

Avoid:
- Wall of text without formatting
- Unrelated information
- Complaints without constructive suggestions
- Duplicate discussions

### Response Guidelines

When responding to discussions:
- Provide helpful, respectful answers
- Include relevant documentation links
- Share working solutions and workarounds
- Acknowledge when you don't know the answer
- Update original post if solution found

---

## Moderation Policy

The Nexus IDE community is moderated to maintain a safe, welcoming space. Discussions may be:

- **Locked**: If discussion becomes off-topic or hostile
- **Moved**: If posted in the wrong category (we'll redirect you)
- **Deleted**: If they violate community guidelines
- **Answered**: By maintainers or helpful community members

For detailed community guidelines, see [CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md](CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)

---

## Getting Started

1. **Read existing discussions** in your category
2. **Use the search** to find similar discussions
3. **Create a clear title** that describes your topic
4. **Provide context** and details in your first message
5. **Be respectful** to all community members
6. **Check back** for responses and engage in discussion

---

## Questions About These Guidelines?

If you have questions about which category to use or these guidelines, feel free to:

- 📧 Email: community@nexus-ide.dev
- 💬 Start a discussion in [Help ❓](https://github.com/Joelito080/nexus-ide/discussions/categories/help)
- 🐦 Follow us on social media for updates

---

**Thank you for being part of the Nexus IDE community! 🚀**
